text: Standing within these angular, glass-enclosed spaces, I feel both exposed and protected; the cool clarity invites analysis but also evokes a sense of surveillance. Moving into the vibrant R+D exhibit, I find myself contemplating the porous boundary between innovation and observation. Surrounded by information and reflective statements, I oscillate between curiosity and self-awareness, questioning my own role as observer and participant in this environment. There is a subdued tension here—a place that urges me to examine not only the technologies on display, but also the act of seeing and being seen.
